title: Verification of two hydrological models for real-time flood forecasting in the Hindu Kush Himalaya (HKH) region
justification: HIWAT-SPT modeled predictions are estimated by routing HIWAT precipitation forecasts through the same RAPID model but using a higher density stream network. The GLoFAS-RAPID probabilistic forecasts are derived through transforming the ensemble set into a binary probability of flood dichotomy weather forecast is verified as flood or non-flood event.

title: The AquiFR hydrometeorological modelling platform as a tool for improving groundwater resource monitoring over France: evaluation over a 60-year period
justification: The AquiFR platform uses "the river routing scheme based on the Routing Application for Parallel computatIon of Discharge (RAPID) model" and also mentions "the RAPID river routing model using a fine-scale river network covering all of France."

title: Comparison of generalized non-data-driven lake and reservoir routing models for global-scale hydrologic forecasting of reservoir outflow at diurnal time steps
justification: The incorporation of D03 and H06 can be considered as modules in large-scale river routing models such as Routing Application for Parallel computatIon of Discharge (RAPID, David et al., 2011).

title: Improved accuracy and efficiency of flood inundation mapping of low-, medium-, and high-flow events using the AutoRoute model
venue: Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences
justification: Recently, AutoRoute has been applied with large-scale river routing models – such as the RAPID model (David et al., 2011; Tavakoly et al., 2017) within SPT – to simulate high-resolution flood inundation maps over large extents
